title: vicia cracca subsp. incana
intro: Vicia cracca subsp. incana, also known as hairy sweet pea or hairy vetch, is a perennial legume native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and easing respiratory problems. Hairy sweet pea can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in salads or as a garnish, although it's essential to note that the plant can be toxic in large quantities.
